summary: Matched the stage utility source files grdatfiles.c and a large portion of ground.c, moving substantial code out of asm/melee/text_2.s into new C sources and stage-specific residual asm files. The PR also expanded stage-related type knowledge, especially StageInfo, StageData, Map, archive-backed stage data, and callback signatures. The author explicitly noted that about a dozen ground.c functions remained inline asm and that some ground data still remained in assembly, so this was a major partial match rather than a complete stage subsystem decompilation.
what_changed: Added src/melee/gr/grdatfiles.c and grdatfiles.h with matched stage DAT/archive utilities, including functions around lbArchive_InitializeDAT, HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, map_head, coll_data, grGroundParam, itemdata, ALDYakuAll, map_ptcl, map_texg, yakumono_param, map_plit, and quake_model_set.
Added src/melee/gr/ground.c with 4771 lines of common ground/stage utility logic and a global StageInfo stage_info definition.

Deleted 13406 lines from asm/melee/text_2.s while adding new residual assembly files under asm/melee/gr for granime.s, grdisplay.s, grmaterial.s, and ground.s, indicating a large relocation from monolithic text assembly into per-module C/asm units.
Added ground.c.o, ground.s.o, grdisplay.s.o, grdatfiles.c.o, granime.s.o, and grmaterial.s.o to obj_files.mk and added grdatfiles.c.o and ground.c.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.

Expanded StageInfo in stage.h from mostly opaque padding into many named offset fields, including camera/vector fields x130 through x16C, callback pointer x178, GObj/JObj arrays, and stage DAT resource pointers x6A8 through x6CC.

Refined StageData and StageCallbacks signatures: callback2 now takes HSD_GObj*, StageData callback1 takes s32, callback5 is BOOL (*)(s32), data1 is char*, and x2C/x30 were identified as an S16Vec array and size.
Introduced StructPairWithStageID as a named typedef in stage.h and updated stage.c locals/globals to use it.
Refined Map fields in stage.h: x8 and xC became HSD_GObj callback pointers, x18 became HSD_GObj*, x1C_callback was typed as a callback, and intermediate storage/padding was adjusted.
Updated stage-specific files such as grstory.c, grshrine.c, and grbattle.c to use the refined Map callback names and BOOL callback signatures, returning FALSE instead of integer 0 where appropriate.

Updated include/functions.h with more accurate prototypes needed by ground/grdatfiles, including func_80015BD0 returning HSD_Archive*, vararg archive loaders, func_801C28AC returning BOOL, func_801C3FA4 returning HSD_JObj*, and several HSD_GObj/HSD_WObj-related signatures.

Adjusted sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h inline accessors to use assert_line and reordered/rewrote HSD_JObjGetRotation, HSD_JObjSetScale, HSD_JObjGetScale, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, HSD_JObjGetTranslation, and HSD_JObjGetMtx forms needed for matching.
Exposed It_Kind_Mato = 0xD1 in item id definitions, likely because ground/stage code references the target item kind.
smart_moves: Kept the match practical by splitting remaining unmatched or data-heavy regions into dedicated asm/melee/gr/*.s files rather than forcing all of text_2.s into C at once.
Documented the partial state clearly in the PR body: approximately a dozen ground.c functions were still inline asm and some ground data section content remained in assembly.
Used offset-based field names in StageInfo, Map, and unknown stage structures where semantics were not yet known, preserving layout evidence without overnaming uncertain fields.
Converted ambiguous integer callbacks to BOOL where the decompiled usage showed boolean behavior, improving type propagation across stage code and callers.

Added resource-name evidence directly into grdatfiles.c by resolving public DAT symbols such as map_head, coll_data, grGroundParam, map_ptcl, and map_texg, which gives future stage decompilation concrete anchor points.
Retained TODO comments for uncertain structures and fields, such as UnkStage6B0 and StageInfo.x12C, instead of inventing semantic names.
Recorded a specific non-match reason for func_801C4E70: an epilogue ordering bug in frank.py, with the C attempt guarded by NON_MATCHING and the matching asm fallback kept.
decomp_lessons: For large stage modules, first carve matched functions and known data-loading logic out of monolithic text assembly, then leave residual assembly in module-named asm files to keep the build organized and searchable.
Resource-loading code is highly useful for reconstructing stage structures because public DAT names reveal field roles more directly than control flow alone.
When matching code depends on callback tables, fixing prototype accuracy across headers and stage-specific modules can be as important as the local C rewrite.
Use BOOL return types once call sites and branch behavior confirm boolean semantics; this can unlock cleaner matching and reduce casts or misleading s32 signatures.
Preserve exact struct layout with xNN field names and padding arrays until stronger semantic evidence appears; the PR gained a lot of structure knowledge without overcommitting names.
Inline HSD accessors can affect codegen; matching may require using the same assert_line form, accessor ordering, and dirty-matrix flag checks as the original headers.
A known toolchain/codegen issue should be isolated with NON_MATCHING plus an asm fallback, with a clear comment explaining the cause, so future contributors can retry when tooling improves.
Build-order changes in obj_files.mk and e_files.mk are part of the match: adding new C objects and residual asm objects in the right sequence is necessary when splitting a section like text_2.s.
naming_conventions: Unknown but layout-important structs used Unk-prefixed names such as UnkBgmStruct, UnkStage6B0, UnkStageDat, and UnkArchiveStruct.
StageInfo fields were named by offset, e.g. x6AC, x6B0, x6B8, x6BC, x130, x13C, x148, x154, x160, x16C, when semantics were incomplete.
Map callback fields were upgraded from x8/xC to x8_callback and xC_callback, and x1C_callback was typed as a function pointer.
StructPairWithStageID was promoted from a local struct in stage.c into a shared typedef in stage.h.
Boolean stage callbacks were named and typed as BOOL-returning, with implementations returning FALSE instead of raw 0.
Item enum naming follows the project pattern It_Kind_*; this PR added It_Kind_Mato for the Target item.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a large block from asm/melee/text_2.s and replaced it with matched C object files plus smaller residual asm files for gr/ground, gr/granime, gr/grdisplay, and gr/grmaterial.
Used NON_MATCHING around a C version of func_801C4E70 and retained inline asm because the stated blocker was an epilogue ordering bug in frank.py.
Kept static helper prototypes in headers where needed for translation-unit matching, including static functions in ground.h and grdatfiles.h.
Matched archive functions using exact public DAT string names and HSD archive calls, which likely stabilized data references and string/data section ordering.
Adjusted baselib JObj inline functions to assert_line-based forms, reducing mismatch risk from different assert expansion or branch structure.
Changed function prototypes globally in include/functions.h to match actual return types and argument types needed by the new C sources, reducing undefined or variadic-call mismatch pressure.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully matched the remaining inline asm functions in src/melee/gr/ground.c, especially func_801C4E70 or functions blocked by frank.py epilogue ordering.
Search for later renames of UnkStage6B0, UnkStageDat, UnkArchiveStruct, and StageInfo x6A8-x6CC resource fields.
Check subsequent PRs touching asm/melee/gr/granime.s, grdisplay.s, grmaterial.s, and ground.s to see which residual assembly blocks were decompiled next.
Look for later review or commits replacing Map x8_callback/xC_callback/x1C_callback names with semantic callback names.
Search for changes to StageInfo.x12C to determine whether the TODO about a GObj/TObj-like color field was resolved.
